title: Disable RDP firewall rule open to 0.0.0.0/0
rollback_instructions: 1. Go to GCP Console → VPC Network → Firewall or use gcloud. 2. Run: gcloud compute firewall-rules update default-allow-rdp --no-disabled --project=local-pointer-489706-f8. 3. Verify the rule is re-enabled: gcloud compute firewall-rules describe default-allow-rdp --project=local-pointer-489706-f8 | grep disabled. 4. Confirm RDP connectivity is restored to any VMs that need it. NOTE: Before re-enabling, strongly consider restricting source ranges to a trusted CIDR instead of 0.0.0.0/0.

title: Restrict SSH firewall rule to IAP proxy range
rollback_instructions: 1. If engineers report they cannot SSH into VMs, the source range can be widened back. 2. Run: gcloud compute firewall-rules update default-allow-ssh --source-ranges=0.0.0.0/0 --project=local-pointer-489706-f8. 3. Verify: gcloud compute firewall-rules describe default-allow-ssh --project=local-pointer-489706-f8 | grep -A5 sourceRanges. 4. IMPORTANT: Restoring 0.0.0.0/0 re-exposes SSH to the entire internet — only do this as a temporary measure and immediately work on restricting to known corporate CIDRs or IAP range (35.235.240.0/20). 5. Ensure engineers use 'gcloud compute ssh --tunnel-through-iap' for IAP-based access.

title: Restrict RDP rule to internal-only as secondary control
rollback_instructions: 1. This only affects the source ranges stored in the rule definition; the rule itself is already disabled. 2. To restore original ranges: gcloud compute firewall-rules update default-allow-rdp --source-ranges=0.0.0.0/0 --project=local-pointer-489706-f8. 3. WARNING: Only restore 0.0.0.0/0 if you have a documented business requirement for public RDP — this is almost never the case.
